Package i5.cb.api

Class CBConnection

  • java.lang.Object
    • i5.cb.api.CBclient
      • i5.cb.api.CBConnection
  • All Implemented Interfaces:
    ICBclient, java.rmi.Remote
    Direct Known Subclasses:
    CBNotificationConnection

    public class CBConnection
extends CBclient
    represents an RMI-able connection to a ConceptBase server

    • Field Summary

      • Fields inherited from class i5.cb.api.CBclient

        USE_LOCAL_CBCLIENT, USE_REMOTE_CBCLIENT, USE_REMOTE_CBCLIENT_IF_NEEDED

    • Constructor Summary

      Constructors 
      Constructor Description
      CBConnection​(CBConnectionData connData)
      opens a connection to a ConceptBase server, either via a local or a remote CBclient object, depending on connData.
      CBConnection​(java.lang.String sHostName, int iPortNo, java.lang.String sToolName, java.lang.String sUserName)
      opens a direct connection to a ConceptBase server, via a local CBclient object.
      CBConnection​(java.lang.String sHostName, int iPortNo, java.lang.String sToolName, java.lang.String sUserName, int iUseCBclient, java.lang.String sRMIServerHostName, int iRMIServerPortNo)
      opens a connection to a ConceptBase server, either via a local or a remote CBclient object, depending on iUseCBclient.

      tells the server to start sending notification messages about a certain issue on a certain notification connection.
      CBanswer startNotifyingAboutView​(java.lang.String sViewName, CBNotificationConnection notifConn)
      tells the server to start sending notification messages about a certain view on a certain notification connection.

      tells the server to stop sending notification messages about a particular issue.
      CBanswer stopNotifyingAboutView​(java.lang.String sViewName, CBNotificationConnection notifConn)
      tells the server to stop sending notification messages about a certain view on a certain notification connection.

      stops the server.

      tells Telos frames to the server

    • Constructor Detail

      • CBConnection

        public CBConnection​(java.lang.String sHostName,
                    int iPortNo,
                    java.lang.String sToolName,
                    java.lang.String sUserName)
             throws CBException
        opens a direct connection to a ConceptBase server, via a local CBclient object. There is no timeout on this connection by default, since the server sometimes computes for minutes or hours, and we don't want to miss the results... ;-)
        Parameters:
        sHostName - name of the host where the server is running
        iPortNo - portno under which the server is running
        sToolName - name of the tool/client (optional)
        sUserName - name of the user who wants to connect (optional)
        Throws:
        CBException

      • CBConnection

        public CBConnection​(CBConnectionData connData)
             throws CBException,
                    java.rmi.RemoteException,
                    java.net.MalformedURLException,
                    java.rmi.NotBoundException
        opens a connection to a ConceptBase server, either via a local or a remote CBclient object, depending on connData. There is no timeout on this connection by default, since the server sometimes computes for minutes or hours, and we don't want to miss the results... ;-)
        Throws:
        CBException
        java.rmi.RemoteException
        java.net.MalformedURLException
        java.rmi.NotBoundException
        See Also:
        CBConnectionData

      • CBConnection

        public CBConnection​(java.lang.String sHostName,
                    int iPortNo,
                    java.lang.String sToolName,
                    java.lang.String sUserName,
                    int iUseCBclient,
                    java.lang.String sRMIServerHostName,
                    int iRMIServerPortNo)
             throws CBException,
                    java.rmi.RemoteException,
                    java.net.MalformedURLException,
                    java.rmi.NotBoundException
        opens a connection to a ConceptBase server, either via a local or a remote CBclient object, depending on iUseCBclient. There is no timeout on this connection by default, since the server sometimes computes for minutes or hours, and we don't want to miss the results... ;-)
        Parameters:
        sHostName - name of the host running CBserver to connect to
        iPortNo - port number under which the CBserver offers its services
        sToolName - tool name under which this client will connect
        sUserName - user name for registering to the CBserver
        iUseCBclient - (see USE_xxx constants)
        sRMIServerHostName - hostname of the RMI server
        iRMIServerPortNo - portno of the RMI server. If == -1, the default portno (1099) is used.
        Throws:
        CBException
        java.rmi.RemoteException
        java.net.MalformedURLException
        java.rmi.NotBoundException

      • tell

        public CBanswer tell​(java.lang.String sFrames)
              throws CBException,
                     java.rmi.RemoteException
        tells Telos frames to the server
        Specified by:

        Overrides:
        tell in class CBclient
        Parameters:
        sFrames - Telos frames (as string)
        Returns:
        a CBanswer object containing the result and the completion

      • retell

        public CBanswer retell​(java.lang.String sUntellFrames,
                       java.lang.String sTellFrames)
                throws CBException,
                       java.rmi.RemoteException
        retells Telos frames to the server, i.e. untells untellFrames and tells tellFrames in one transaction.
        Specified by:
        retell in interface ICBclient
        Overrides:
        retell in class CBclient
        Parameters:
        sUntellFrames - Telos frames to untell
        sTellFrames - Telos frames to tell
        Throws:
        CBException - if an error occurs while processing this method
        java.rmi.RemoteException
        See Also:
        ICBclient.tell(String), ICBclient.untell(String)

      • ask

        public CBanswer ask​(java.lang.String sQuery,
                    java.lang.String sQueryFormat,
                    java.lang.String sAnswerRep,
                    java.lang.String sRollbackTime)
             throws CBException,
                    java.rmi.RemoteException
        asks the server something. An empty answer is represented as "nil". In contrast to CBclient.ask, if answerRep == "FRAME" or answerRep == "LABEL", an empty answer is represented by the empty string (""), rather than the string "nil". In all other cases, an empty answer is represented as "nil". Because of a conceptual error in ConceptBase, when using the answer representation "LABEL", an empty answer cannot be distinguished from the answer containing only the object nil. This function cannot fix this. The only solution is to avoid using the object called nil in the database.
        Specified by:
        ask in interface ICBclient
        Overrides:
        ask in class CBclient
        Parameters:
        sQuery - the query to ask
        sQueryFormat - the format of the query (must be FRAMES or OBJNAMES)
        sAnswerRep - the desired answer representation, e.g. FRAME, LABEL, VIEW
        sRollbackTime - the rollback time (e.g. Now)
        Returns:
        a CBanswer object containing the result and the completion
        Throws:
        CBException - if an error occurs while processing this method
        java.rmi.RemoteException
        See Also:
        askFrame( String, String ), askObjectName( String, String )

      • askFrame

        public CBanswer askFrame​(java.lang.String sQueryAsFrame,
                         java.lang.String sAnswerRep)
                  throws CBException,
                         java.rmi.RemoteException
        ask query as frame; rollbacktime is "Now".
        Parameters:
        sQueryAsFrame - the query as a Telos frame
        sAnswerRep - the desired answer representation
        Throws:
        CBException
        java.rmi.RemoteException

      • askObjectName

        public CBanswer askObjectName​(java.lang.String sQueryAsObjectName,
                              java.lang.String sAnswerRep)
                       throws CBException,
                              java.rmi.RemoteException
        ask query as objectname; rollbacktime is "Now".
        Parameters:
        sQueryAsObjectName - the query as an objectname (must be told before)
        sAnswerRep - the desired answer representation
        Throws:
        CBException
        java.rmi.RemoteException

      • getFrame

        public java.lang.String getFrame​(java.lang.String sObjectName)
                          throws CBException,
                                 java.rmi.RemoteException
        asks for the frame representation of an object. (Calls the builtin query get_object.)
        Parameters:
        sObjectName - name of the object
        Returns:
        the frame representation of the object
        Throws:
        CBException
        java.rmi.RemoteException

      • getErrorMessages

        public final java.lang.String getErrorMessages​()
                                        throws CBException,
                                               java.rmi.RemoteException
        gets the error messages concerning the last operation. This should be called when CBanswer.getCompletion() == CBanswer.ERROR
        Specified by:
        getErrorMessages in interface ICBclient
        Overrides:
        getErrorMessages in class CBclient
        Returns:
        the error message(s) from the server
        Throws:
        CBException - if an error occurs while processing this method
        java.rmi.RemoteException

      • stopServer

        public CBanswer stopServer​()
                    throws CBException,
                           java.rmi.RemoteException
        stops the server. This is only allowed for the same user who started the server, and on the host where the server was started.
        Specified by:
        stopServer in interface ICBclient
        Overrides:
        stopServer in class CBclient
        Returns:
        can be ignored. If the server can't be stopped, an exception is thrown. We have to return an CBanswer though, because our superclass does.
        Throws:
        CBException - if an error occurs while processing this method
        java.rmi.RemoteException

      • startNotifyingAbout

        public CBanswer startNotifyingAbout​(java.lang.String sIssue,
                                    CBNotificationConnection notifConn)
                             throws CBException,
                                    java.rmi.RemoteException
        tells the server to start sending notification messages about a certain issue on a certain notification connection. [Wahrscheinlich sollte hier void returned werden!]
        Parameters:
        sIssue - must have the form type(name), e.g. view(MyView)
        notifConn - the notification connection
        Throws:
        CBException
        java.rmi.RemoteException

      • stopNotifyingAbout

        public CBanswer stopNotifyingAbout​(java.lang.String sIssue,
                                   CBNotificationConnection notifConn)
                            throws CBException,
                                   java.rmi.RemoteException
        tells the server to stop sending notification messages about a particular issue.
        Parameters:
        sIssue - must have the form type(name), e.g. view(MyView)
        notifConn - the notification connection
        Throws:
        CBException
        java.rmi.RemoteException

        notifConn - the notification connection

        public CBanswer stopNotifyingAboutView​(java.lang.String sViewName,
